verses.text: Nebuchadnezzar sets up a golden image and gathers all his rulers to its dedication. Pride in display. The king who heard of God's kingdom now demands worship of his own.
Nebuchadnezzar sets up a golden image and gathers all his rulers to its dedication. Pride in display. The king who heard of God's kingdom now demands worship of his own.
Nebuchadnezzar sets up a golden image and gathers all his rulers to its dedication. Pride in display. The king who heard of God's kingdom now demands worship of his own.
At the music all must fall and worship or be cast into the furnace; the crowds comply. Mass conformity. Most will bow to power rather than risk the flames.
At the music all must fall and worship or be cast into the furnace; the crowds comply. Mass conformity. Most will bow to power rather than risk the flames.

At the music all must fall and worship or be cast into the furnace; the crowds comply. Mass conformity. Most will bow to power rather than risk the flames.
Certain Chaldeans accuse the three Jews who will not serve the king's gods nor worship the image. Envy and malice. Faithfulness always draws the notice of accusers.

The furious king offers another chance, scoffing, 'Who is that God that shall deliver you out of my hands?' Defiant pride. He sets himself against the Almighty without knowing it.
The furious king offers another chance, scoffing, 'Who is that God that shall deliver you out of my hands?' Defiant pride. He sets himself against the Almighty without knowing it.
The furious king offers another chance, scoffing, 'Who is that God that shall deliver you out of my hands?' Defiant pride. He sets himself against the Almighty without knowing it.
They answer that their God is able to deliver them, 'but if not... we will not serve thy gods.' Faith without conditions. They will obey God whether He rescues them or not.
They answer that their God is able to deliver them, 'but if not... we will not serve thy gods.' Faith without conditions. They will obey God whether He rescues them or not.
They answer that their God is able to deliver them, 'but if not... we will not serve thy gods.' Faith without conditions. They will obey God whether He rescues them or not.
The king heats the furnace sevenfold; the flame slays those who cast them in, and the three fall down bound. Rage overreaches. Man's fury cannot touch what God protects.

The king heats the furnace sevenfold; the flame slays those who cast them in, and the three fall down bound. Rage overreaches. Man's fury cannot touch what God protects.
Astonished, the king sees four men loose in the fire, 'and the form of the fourth is like the Son of God.' Divine company. God meets His own in the very heart of the trial.
Astonished, the king sees four men loose in the fire, 'and the form of the fourth is like the Son of God.' Divine company. God meets His own in the very heart of the trial.
He calls them out; the fire had no power, not a hair singed, nor the smell of smoke. Untouched. God's deliverance is complete, leaving no mark of the flame.
He calls them out; the fire had no power, not a hair singed, nor the smell of smoke. Untouched. God's deliverance is complete, leaving no mark of the flame.
The king blesses their God, who 'sent his angel, and delivered his servants that trusted in him,' and promotes them. A pagan moved. Their faith won a witness from the throne itself.
